texts.value: Acts as an electroneutral and low-affinity sodium (Na(+))-dependent sodium-coupled solute transporter (PubMed:17692818). Catalyzes the transport across the plasma membrane of many monocarboxylates such as lactate, pyruvate, nicotinate, propionate, butyrate and beta-D-hydroxybutyrate (By similarity). May be responsible for the first step of reabsorption of monocarboxylates from the lumen of the proximal tubule of the kidney and the small intestine. May play also a role in monocarboxylates transport in the retina (By similarity)
Cotransport of monocarboxylates and nicotinate strongly inhibited by ibuprofen, fenoprofen and ketoprofen
Detected at the brush border membrane of the kidney. Colocalizes with viementin in Mueller cells
Belongs to the sodium:solute symporter (SSF) (TC 2.A.21) family

citation.title: Cloning and functional characterization of human SMCT2 (SLC5A12) and expression pattern of the transporter in kidney.
Sequence of a novel human sodium-solute cotransporter similar to the sodium-iodide transporter.
Human chromosome 11 DNA sequence and analysis including novel gene identification.
The status, quality, and expansion of the NIH full-length cDNA project: the Mammalian Gene Collection (MGC).

properties.value: 3 GO annotations based on evolutionary models
Expressed in adult mammalian kidney and 76 other cell types or tissues
